channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Thu, 18 Jun 2026 10:23:16 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: ‘To be negotiated’: US–Iran interim deal defers final decision on Tehran’s nuclear future
item.description: The United States and Iran have reached an interim agreement on Tehran’s nuclear programme, setting in motion a two-month negotiating window aimed at resolving the core dispute between the two countries over Iran’s nuclear ambitions. The United States and Iran have reached an interim agreement on Tehran’s nuclear programme, setting in motion a two-month negotiating window aimed at resolving the core dispute between the two countries over Iran’s nuclear ambitions.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘Like nuclear, they don't blow up planet’: Trump's U-turn on Iran missiles after deal takes effect
item.description: President Trump defended a new US-Iran understanding on ballistic missiles, deeming it unfair for Tehran to be disarmed if regional rivals possess them. He differentiated missile threats from nuclear ones, suggesting a less urgent global risk. US forces will remain in the Gulf post-agreement, with follow-up talks planned.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Watch: Trump signs US-Iran deal MoU during dinner with Macron, says 'this was not easy'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Chud the Builder’s bond revoked after new footage emerges in Tennessee shooting case
item.description: Chud the Builder’s bond in a Davidson County misdemeanor case was revoked after investigators presented footage that allegedly shows him shooting Joshua Fox before being attacked. The evidence challenges his self-defense claim in the May 2026 courthouse shooting. With serious felony charges already pending, Eatherly remains in jail as both cases move through the court system.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: "I was pressured into unideal agreements": Clavicular's Miami nightclub investment turns into explosive legal battle over unpaid profits
item.description: Clavicular has spoken out amid his legal dispute with Miami's Bacara Club, claiming he was "pressured into unideal agreements" after his lawyer alleged he never received profits from a $400,000 investment. While the nightclub's representatives dispute the claims, Clavicular insists the matter will be resolved. The dispute has sparked fresh questions over ownership, unpaid earnings, and the influencer's business partnerships.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: 'It's signed': US-Iran MoU officially finalized after both sides digitally sign agreement
item.description: US President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian have virtually signed a 14-point memorandum of understanding to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The agreement, effective immediately, also initiates negotiations on sanctions relief and Iran's nuclear program, marking a significant de-escalation.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: "Skipped Charlie's memorial despite being invited": Candace Owens accuses Blake Neff of proving Charlie Kirk 'was betrayed' in explosive feud
item.description: Candace Owens escalated her public feud with Blake Neff by challenging him to a livestream debate over Charlie Kirk's case, claiming Kirk believed in open discussion. She also accused Neff of fueling the belief that Charlie "was betrayed." Neff fired back, alleging Owens skipped discussions and Kirk's memorial. Their exchange has reignited online debate over Kirk's legacy and deepened divisions among conservative commentators.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: ExtraEmily addresses backlash after Columbia University resume reveal leaves supporters and critics sharply divided
item.description: ExtraEmily responded to critics after a resume showcase clip gained attention online. While some viewers mocked her behavior, many praised her strong academic record, Columbia University achievements, and professional experience. The streamer defended herself during a June 16 broadcast, saying her accomplishments are often overlooked because of the exaggerated personality she displays on stream.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Nina Lin appears permanently banned from Twitch days after NYC arrest during Knicks celebrations
item.description: Twitch streamer Nina Lin's channel is now offline following her arrest during New York Knicks' championship celebrations. Videos showed her on an Amazon van roof before police intervened. This ban, indicated by a "unavailable" message, follows previous suspensions for controversies involving resurfaced clips and a viral Target livestream, renewing scrutiny of her platform history.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Liverpool man made over £300,000 by doing students’ exams, coursework for cash
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indian-American Jaswinder Singh accused of identity fraud faces US citizenship revocation
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: IShowSpeed's reaction steals the show after Portugal score their first goal at the 2026 World Cup
item.description: IShowSpeed's enthusiastic reaction to Portugal's opening goal in the 2026 FIFA World Cup match against DR Congo captured widespread attention. The streamer's passionate celebration quickly went viral online. Fans enjoyed his energetic display, which has become a hallmark of his football content. This moment highlights Speed's significant influence in the online football community.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: 'Go back to India': Indian-American founder opens up about 8-year immigrant journey
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Who is Aman Sanger? Indian-American whose AI startup could boost his net worth to $2.6 billion
Australian woman dies after blood loss during home birth after refusing ambulance assistance
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘Nobody did that on purpose’: Trump says Iran girls’ school bombing was not deliberate
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 8 best places to retire on the Pacific Coast: Affordable coastal towns in Washington, Oregon and California for peaceful senior living
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: The continent that keeps the dead: Antarctica’s most disturbing unsolved disappearances and frozen expedition mysteries
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Boss baby: Trump says “I’m the boss” as critics say he’s getting bossed by Iran
item.description: President Trump, facing criticism for a potential Iran deal, declared himself "the boss" while threatening renewed military action. Critics argue the proposed Memorandum of Understanding offers Iran significant concessions, including sanctions relief and asset unfreezing, in exchange for vague nuclear assurances. This has sparked domestic debate and bipartisan opposition in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Chidanand Rajghatta

item.title: Customer calls police on father who accompanied two young daughters to women's washroom
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
